item.title: 24th Sunday after Pentecost, The Healing Of The Woman With An Issue Of Blood And The Raising Of The Daughter Of Jairus, Who touched me?, 2008-11-30

item.title: 24th Sunday after Pentecost, For he is our peace and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us; Having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances., She touched the border of his garment: and immediately her issue of blood stanched., 2009-11-22
item.description: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. For he is our peace and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us; Having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances., She touched the border of his garment: and immediately her issue of blood stanched., 2009-11-22
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. For he is our peace and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us; Having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances., She touched the border of his garment: and immediately her issue of blood stanched., 2009-11-22

item.title: 24th Sunday after Pentecost, He Is Our Peace, 2010-11-07
item.description: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. The Gospel is exactly proclaimed in this passage from Ephesians. The entire passage is discussed, especially "For he is our peace, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us; 15 Having abolished in his flesh the enmity...". What is peace accordig to the Gospel? What did Christ do so we can obtain it? What must we do? What two things are at enmity with each other?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. The Gospel is exactly proclaimed in this passage from Ephesians. The entire passage is discussed, especially "For he is our peace, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us; 15 Having abolished in his flesh the enmity...". What is peace accordig to the Gospel? What did Christ do so we can obtain it? What must we do? What two things are at enmity with each other?

item.title: 24th Sunday after Pentecost, Having Abolished In His Flesh The Enmity Even The Law Of Commandments Contained In Ordinances, 2011-11-26
item.description: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. Having Abolished In His Flesh The Enmity Even The Law Of Commandments Contained In Ordinances, 2011-11-26
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. Having Abolished In His Flesh The Enmity Even The Law Of Commandments Contained In Ordinances, 2011-11-26

item.title: 24th Sunday after Pentecost, 2012-11-18
item.description: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. The number 12 appears twice in the story of the raising of the daughter of Jairus and the healing of the woman with an issue of blood; this is not accidental. We see two kinds of faith - weak and strong, and the good news that the Lord will respond to even to weak faith, but we must also respond when He does! Of course, the whole point of His response to our weak faith is that it would become stronger! We look at the weak faith of Jairus and his necessary response to the Lord, and learn form the strong faith of the woman with an issue of blood.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :24th Sunday after Pentecost. The number 12 appears twice in the story of the raising of the daughter of Jairus and the healing of the woman with an issue of blood; this is not accidental. We see two kinds of faith - weak and strong, and the good news that the Lord will respond to even to weak faith, but we must also respond when He does! Of course, the whole point of His response to our weak faith is that it would become stronger! We look at the weak faith of Jairus and his necessary response to the Lord, and learn form the strong faith of the woman with an issue of blood.
